sourceMovie.title: Criminal
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Criminal (2016) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Face/Off
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Criminal clicked for you, Face/Off (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Inception
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Criminal clicked for you, Inception (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Equalizer 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Criminal clicked for you, The Equalizer 2 (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Criminal?
faq.answer: Try Face/Off, Inception, The Equalizer 2—each shares DNA with Criminal while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Criminal?
faq.answer: Criminal (2016) is best described as Action, Crime, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Criminal worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
